name: Grilled Cheese Pita Pockets
recipeIngredient: 1 oz Sliced, reduced-fat cheese (Jarisberg, Monterey Jack)
3 Bell pepper rings (red, yellow, or green), paper-thin with seeds and stems removed
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut a thin strip from one side of the pita.
2. Carefully open the pita to create a pocket for the filling.
3. Place one slice of cheese in the pita pocket.
4. Slide in the tomato slices; add the alfalfa sprouts and top with the pickle slices and pepper rings.
5. Add the remaining cheese slice.
6. Heat a medium (8-10") skillet over medium-high heat until hot enough to evaporate a drop of water upon contact.
7. Spray with vegetable-oil cooking spray.
8. Add the sandwich; cook, covered, over medium-low heat until browned on both sides, turning once, about 10 minutes.
Nutritional analysis per serving: 206 calories; 7 grams total fat; 13 grams protein; 25 grams carbohydrates; 19 milligrams cholesterol; 601 milligrams sodium.
